LanguageData.VCE_AgaveNectar.description: A thin, semi-sweet nectar pressed from Nevada agave. It keeps the soothing sting of the desert plant while working as a versatile wasteland sweetener.
LanguageData.VCE_Allspice.description: A hardy patch of broc flowers. Their petals bring a peppery, herbal bite to wasteland stews, grills, and sauces.
LanguageData.VCE_Allspice.label: broc flower patch
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.descrip: A rare pre-war restorative suspension packaged as a culinary additive. Folded into food, aqua cura can pull a body back from death by flooding it with emergency repair agents.
LanguageData.VCE_DigestibleResurrectorNanites.label: aqua cura
LanguageData.VCE_Flour.description: Ground razorgrain flour used for breads, pies, and bakery work across the wasteland.
LanguageData.VCE_Flour.label: razorgrain flour
LanguageData.VCE_InsectJellyPreserves.description: A sticky batch of preserves made from giant ant jelly. Sweet, strange, and somehow still good on toast.
LanguageData.VCE_InsectJellyPreserves.label: ant preserves
LanguageData.VCE_RawSpices.description: Dried broc flower petals prepared for seasoning soups, meats, and sauces.
LanguageData.VCE_RawSpices.label: broc flower petals
LanguageData.VCE_Sugarcane.description: A tall stand of maize bred for dense, syrup-rich kernels. It is milled down into the sweeteners used throughout pre-war kitchens and diner recipes.
LanguageData.VCE_Sugarcane.label: maize stalk
LanguageData.VCE_Wheat.description: A tough amber cereal bred from razorgrain. It yields the coarse flour used in wasteland breads, pastries, and campfire cooking.
LanguageData.VCE_Wheat.label: razorgrain stalk
